segments.text: Give ear, O heavens, and I will speak; hear, O earth, the words of my mouth.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Let my teaching fall like rain and my speech settle like dew, like gentle rain on new grass, like showers on tender plants.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: For I will proclaim the name of the LORD. Ascribe greatness to our God!
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: He is the Rock, His work is perfect; all His ways are just. A God of faithfulness without injustice, righteous and upright is He.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text:
His people have acted corruptly toward Him; the spot on them is not that of His children,
but of a perverse and crooked generation.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Is this how you repay the LORD, O foolish and senseless people? Is He not your Father and Creator? Has He not made you and established you?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Remember the days of old; consider the years long past. Ask your father, and he will tell you, your elders, and they will inform you.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance, when He divided the sons of man, He set the boundaries of the peoplesaccording to the number of the sons of God.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But the LORD’s portion is His people, Jacob His allotted inheritance.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: He found him in a desert land, in a barren, howling wilderness; He surrounded him, He instructed him, He guarded him as the apple of His eye.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: As an eagle stirs up its nest and hovers over its young, He spread His wings to catch them and carried them on His pinions.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The LORD alone led him, and no foreign god was with him.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: He made him ride on the heights of the land and fed him the produce of the field. He nourished him with honey from the rock and oil from the flinty crag,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: with curds from the herd and milk from the flock, with the fat of lambs, with rams from Bashan, and goats, with the choicest grains of wheat. From the juice of the finest grapesyou drank the wine.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But Jeshurun grew fat and kicked—becoming fat, bloated, and gorged. He abandoned the God who made him and scorned the Rock of his salvation.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: They provoked His jealousy with foreign gods; they enraged Him with abominations.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: They sacrificed to demons, not to God, to gods they had not known, to newly arrived gods, which your fathers did not fear.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: You ignored the Rock who brought you forth; you forgot the God who gave you birth.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: When the LORD saw this, He rejected them, provoked to anger by His sons and daughters.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: He said:
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: I will hide My face from them; I will see what will be their end. For they are a perverse generation—children of unfaithfulness.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They have provoked My jealousy by that which is not God; they have enraged Me with their worthless idols. So I will make them jealous by those who are not a people; I will make them angry by a nation without understanding.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: For a fire has been kindled by My anger, and it burns to the depths of Sheol; it consumes the earth and its produce, and scorches the foundations of the mountains.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: I will heap disasters upon them; I will spend My arrows against them.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: They will be wasted from hunger and ravaged by pestilence and bitter plague; I will send the fangs of wild beasts against them, with the venom of vipers that slither in the dust.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Outside, the sword will take their children, and inside, terror will strikethe young man and the young woman, the infant and the gray-haired man.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: I would have said that I would cut them to pieces and blot out their memory from mankind,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: if I had not dreaded the taunt of the enemy, lest their adversaries misunderst and and say:‘Our own hand has prevailed; it was not the LORD who did all this.’
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Israel is a nation devoid of counsel, with no understanding among them.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: If only they were wise, they would understand it; they would comprehend their fate.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: How could one man pursue a thousand, or two put ten thousand to flight, unless their Rock had sold them, unless the LORD had given them up?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: For their rock is not like our Rock, even our enemies concede.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But their vine is from the vine of Sodom and from the fields of Gomorrah. Their grapes are poisonous; their clusters are bitter.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Their wine is the venom of serpents, the deadly poison of cobras.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Have I not stored up these things, sealed up within My vaults?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Vengeance is Mine; I will repay. In due time their foot will slip; for their day of disaster is near, and their doom is coming quickly.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: For the LORD will vindicate His people and have compassion on His servantswhen He sees that their strength is gone and no one remains, slave or free.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: He will say:
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Where are their gods, the rock in which they took refuge,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: which ate the fat of their sacrifices and drank the wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise up and help you; let them give you shelter!
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: See now that I am He; there is no God besides Me. I bring death and I give life; I wound and I heal, and there is no one who can deliver from My hand.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: For I lift up My hand to heaven and declare: As surely as I live forever,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: when I sharpen My flashing sword, and My hand grasps it in judgment, I will take vengeance on My adversaries and repay those who hate Me.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: I will make My arrows drunk with blood, while My sword devours flesh—the blood of the slain and captives, the heads of the enemy leaders.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Rejoice, O heavens, with Him, and let all God’s angels worship Him. Rejoice, O nations, with His people; for He will avenge the blood of His children. He will take vengeance on His adversaries and repay those who hate Him; He will cleanse His land and His people.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Then Moses came with Joshua son of Nun and recited all the words of this song in the hearing of the people.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: When Moses had finished reciting all these words to all Israel,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: he said to them,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Take to heart all these words I testify among you today, so that you may command your children to carefully follow all the words of this law.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: For they are not idle words to you, because they are your life, and by them you will live long in the land that you are crossing the Jordan to possess.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: On that same day the LORD said to Moses,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Go up into the Abarim Range to Mount Nebo, in the land of Moab across from Jericho, and view the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites as their own possession.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And there on the mountain that you climb, you will die and be gathered to your people, just as your brother Aaron died on Mount Hor and was gathered to his people.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For at the waters of Meribah-kadesh in the Wilderness of Zin, both of you broke faith with Me among the Israelites by failing to treat Me as holy in their presence.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Although you shall see from a distance the land that I am giving the Israelites, you shall not enter it.
segments.speaker: The LORD
